description: Benchmark map for comparing DbSqlLikeMem capabilities and performance against real/native database engines.

providers.notes: Use SQL Server as the nearest real-engine proxy in local benchmarks.
Change the image tag here if you prefer to stay strictly inside the Db2 11 family.

features.notes: Measures the overhead to create and open a fresh provider connection.
Measures the cost to create the base benchmark schema and required objects.
Measures the cost to create the benchmark users and orders tables with a foreign-key relationship.
Measures the cost to create the foreign-key tables and insert a referenced row.
Measures the cost to drop the benchmark users table created by the DDL workflow.
Measures a single-row insert path end-to-end.
Measures inserting rows starting from a custom id.
New benchmark for smaller batch size to complement InsertBatch100.
Measures the insertion of one hundred rows in a sequential batch workload.
Priority benchmark to measure concurrent batch insert throughput.
Measures point lookup by primary key.
Measures a representative joined read query across benchmark tables.
Measures updating one row identified by primary key.
Measures deleting one row identified by primary key.
Measures transaction commit overhead for a small write workload.
Measures transaction rollback overhead for a small write workload.
Benchmark for SAVEPOINT creation cost and transaction state tracking.
Benchmark for rollback-to-savepoint and snapshot restoration.
Benchmark for RELEASE SAVEPOINT handling.
Benchmark for nested savepoint orchestration in realistic transactional flows.
Measures the provider-specific upsert path.
Benchmark for binding a broad set of parameter types in a scalar projection.
Benchmark for binding parameters in a single-row INSERT.
Benchmark for calling a registered stored procedure in the mock runtime.
Measures obtaining the next value from a database sequence or equivalent.
Multi-statement / runner-oriented batch insert scenario.
Multi-statement / runner-oriented batch insert 100 scenario.
Mixed DML and query batch flow closer to real application scripts.
ExecuteReader and multi-result batch flow.
Multi-statement scalar pipeline benchmark.
Benchmark for pure non-query batch execution.
Batch flow with BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK or equivalent transaction control.
Parser-focused benchmark to isolate simple SELECT parsing cost.
Parser-focused benchmark for larger join trees and predicates.
Parser benchmark for INSERT ... RETURNING flows.
Parser benchmark for PostgreSQL/SQLite style UPSERT syntax handling.
Parser benchmark for JSON path extraction expressions.
Parser benchmark for ordered-set aggregate syntax.
Parser benchmark for automatic pagination dialect adaptation.
Parser benchmark for multi-statement scripts and batch splitting.
JSON_VALUE / JSON_EXTRACT / operator-based scalar access.
Path-oriented JSON extraction with nested traversal.
JSON insert/extract flow including type coercion and cast overhead.
Measures provider string aggregation with default ordering/shape.
Ordered STRING_AGG / GROUP_CONCAT / LISTAGG benchmark.
DISTINCT aggregate concatenation benchmark.
Aggregate concatenation with custom separator benchmark.
Large-group concatenation benchmark to stress aggregation engine.
Measures a representative scalar date/time expression.
CURRENT_TIMESTAMP / NOW / GETDATE / SYSTIMESTAMP benchmark.
DATEADD / DATE_ADD / TIMESTAMPADD cross-dialect benchmark.
Predicate evaluation with current time function.
Sort/order benchmark involving current time expressions.
ROWCOUNT/ROW_COUNT/CHANGES style post-insert tracking.
ROWCOUNT/ROW_COUNT/CHANGES style post-update tracking.
FOUND_ROWS()/rowcount-style post-select tracking.
Rowcount tracking across batch statements.
Backlog benchmark for common table expressions.
Backlog benchmark for ROW_NUMBER window function.
Backlog benchmark for LAG/analytic window processing.
Complex comparison benchmark for EXISTS subqueries over users/orders datasets.
Complex comparison benchmark for correlated subquery aggregation.
Complex comparison benchmark for grouped aggregates with HAVING.
Complex comparison benchmark for UNION ALL projections.
Complex comparison benchmark for DISTINCT projection and deduplication.
Complex comparison benchmark for multi-join aggregate workloads.
Backlog benchmark for PIVOT/UNPIVOT style transformation.
INSERT ... RETURNING / OUTPUT benchmark.
UPDATE ... RETURNING / OUTPUT benchmark.
Backlog benchmark for MERGE-based upsert flows.
Backlog benchmark for partition-pruned read paths.
Complex query benchmark using a scalar sub-select.
Complex query benchmark using an IN sub-select predicate.
Complex query benchmark using CROSS APPLY or LATERAL semantics.
Complex query benchmark using OUTER APPLY or LEFT LATERAL semantics.
Backlog benchmark for LEAD/analytic window processing.
Backlog benchmark for ranking window functions.
Backlog benchmark for first/last value window functions.
Backlog benchmark for NTILE distribution windows.
Backlog benchmark for percentile distribution windows.
Backlog benchmark for NTH_VALUE window function.
Complex query benchmark using NOT EXISTS with a correlated subquery.
Complex query benchmark using LEFT JOIN ... IS NULL anti-join semantics.
Complex query benchmark combining scalar subqueries and CASE expressions.
Complex query benchmark for UNION over compatible projections.
Complex query benchmark using a NOT IN sub-select predicate.
Combined predicate and ordering benchmark using BETWEEN, LIKE, and ORDER BY.
Complex query benchmark for ordered pagination across dialects.
